free_text: The 10,11B(p,n)10,11C reactions between E(p)=13.7 and
14.7 MeV
Pelletron of the Univ. of Sao Paulo
The boron targets were made by centrifuging a colloidal
suspension on the enriched isotope onto a Mylar film
Targets used in the angular distributions at Ep=14. MeV
were: 10B - 1.84 mg/cm2 and 11B - 2.40 mg/cm2 thick;
Targets used in the angular distributions at Ep=14.3MeV
and 14.6 MeV and in the excitation curves from Ep=13.6
to 14.7 MeV were: 10B - 1.24 mg/cm2 and
11B - 2.89 mg/cm2 thick.
NE-213 liquid scintillator 12.7 cm diam, 2.54 cm
Target monitoring was performed by counting neutron
events, within a predetermined energy window, which
occurred in a small NE-111 plastic scintillator placed
close to the target at 90 deg to the beam direction.
Error in target thickness.
Error in the determination of the
neutron peak areas (yields) contributes 1 to 10%.
Error in the precision of the charge
integration circuit that determined the number of
incident particles.
Uncertainty associated with the
determination of the detector solid angle.
Error in the calculated neutron detection
efficiencies contribute 5 to 8%.
The overall uncertainty of 12 to 17% are
shown in several points on figs
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year, lower case
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
